prompt:
Vendor the Darwin Kernel into this repo via git subtree (canonical path) and activate Wire 1 + Wire 2. CONTEXT: a lightweight copy-vendor already exists at vendor/darwin-kernel and additive adapters exist at server/utils/darwin/{govern.ts,capabilities.ts}; a working proof is scripts/darwin-metering-proof.ts. Your job is to make it canonical + wired, behind the normal verify/PR gate.

SCOPE (do ONLY this, keep the diff small):
1) Replace the copy-vendored vendor/darwin-kernel with a real git subtree of the kernel package from the orchestrator repo. Prefer: `git subtree split -P packages/darwin-kernel -b _kernel_split` in the orchestrator, then in THIS repo `git subtree add --prefix vendor/darwin-kernel <orchestrator-remote> _kernel_split --squash`. If a remote isn't available, keep the copy-vendor and add scripts/vendor-darwin-kernel.sh to CI instead — do NOT block on this.
2) Add `"@darwin/kernel": "file:vendor/darwin-kernel"` to package.json dependencies (additive).
3) Wire 1: in server/utils/agentLedger.js, before an action transitions to awaiting_approval/approved, call `govern()` from server/utils/darwin/govern.ts and persist the returned receipt to a new `darwin_receipts` row (append-only). Do NOT change existing tier logic — this is additive instrumentation. Keep Tier-C signed-token flow intact.
4) Wire 2: add a one-time startup/seed (server plugin or script) that calls publishAndMeter()/publishes the Pareto capability specs so monte_carlo/allocator/deduction_optimizer/geo_arbitrage are discoverable.
5) Apply vendor/darwin-kernel/sql/0001_darwin_kernel.sql to the Supabase project (additive darwin_* tables, RLS-enabled). Name-check first.

PROOF THAT IT WORKS (the test you must run and show green):
  node --experimental-strip-types scripts/darwin-metering-proof.ts
  -> must print real engine output (p50Age, paths) + signed+verified usage records + a settlement + capability economics.
Also run the existing Pareto test suite (npx vitest run OR npm test) and confirm no regressions (696 baseline).

GUARDRAILS: additive only; no change to existing route behavior; do not touch other repos; set DARWIN_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY_PEM via env (do not hardcode). Open a PR titled 'Darwin Kernel: vendor + Wire 1/2 (governance receipts + metered capability registry)'. This is a MATERIAL change (touches agentLedger + adds a dependency + DB migration) so it must wait for human approval before merge.
